types.name: RealtimeCreateClientSecretRequest

fields.name: expires_after
fields.description:
Configuration for the client secret expiration. Expiration refers to the time after which
a client secret will no longer be valid for creating sessions. The session itself may
continue after that time once started. A secret can be used to create multiple sessions
until it expires.

fields.name: session
fields.description:
Session configuration to use for the client secret. Choose either a realtime
session or a transcription session.

types.description:
Create a session and client secret for the Realtime API. The request can specify
either a realtime or a transcription session configuration.
[Learn more about the Realtime API](/docs/guides/realtime).

types.name: RealtimeCreateClientSecretRequestExpiresAfter

fields.name: anchor
fields.description: The anchor point for the client secret expiration, meaning that `seconds` will be added to the `created_at` time of the client secret to produce an expiration timestamp. Only `created_at` is currently supported.

fields.name: seconds
fields.description: The number of seconds from the anchor point to the expiration. Select a value between `10` and `7200` (2 hours). This default to 600 seconds (10 minutes) if not specified.

types.description:
Configuration for the client secret expiration. Expiration refers to the time after which
a client secret will no longer be valid for creating sessions. The session itself may
continue after that time once started. A secret can be used to create multiple sessions
until it expires.

types.name: RealtimeCreateClientSecretRequestSession
types.description:
Session configuration to use for the client secret. Choose either a realtime
session or a transcription session.

types.name: RealtimeSessionCreateRequestGa

fields.name: type
fields.description: The type of session to create. Always `realtime` for the Realtime API.

fields.name: output_modalities
fields.description:
The set of modalities the model can respond with. It defaults to `["audio"]`, indicating
that the model will respond with audio plus a transcript. `["text"]` can be used to make
the model respond with text only. It is not possible to request both `text` and `audio` at the same time.

fields.name: model
fields.description: The Realtime model used for this session.

fields.name: instructions
fields.description:
The default system instructions (i.e. system message) prepended to model calls. This field allows the client to guide the model on desired responses. The model can be instructed on response content and format, (e.g. "be extremely succinct", "act friendly", "here are examples of good responses") and on audio behavior (e.g. "talk quickly", "inject emotion into your voice", "laugh frequently"). The instructions are not guaranteed to be followed by the model, but they provide guidance to the model on the desired behavior.

Note that the server sets default instructions which will be used if this field is not set and are visible in the `session.created` event at the start of the session.

fields.name: audio
fields.description: Configuration for input and output audio.

fields.name: include
fields.description:
Additional fields to include in server outputs.

`item.input_audio_transcription.logprobs`: Include logprobs for input audio transcription.

fields.name: tracing
fields.description:
Realtime API can write session traces to the [Traces Dashboard](https://platform.openai.com/logs?api=traces). Set to null to disable tracing. Once
tracing is enabled for a session, the configuration cannot be modified.

`auto` will create a trace for the session with default values for the
workflow name, group id, and metadata.

fields.name: tools
fields.description: Tools available to the model.

fields.name: tool_choice
fields.description:
How the model chooses tools. Provide one of the string modes or force a specific
function/MCP tool.

fields.name: parallel_tool_calls
fields.description:
Whether the model may call multiple tools in parallel. Only supported by
reasoning Realtime models such as `gpt-realtime-2`.

fields.name: max_output_tokens
fields.description:
Maximum number of output tokens for a single assistant response,
inclusive of tool calls. Provide an integer between 1 and 4096 to
limit output tokens, or `inf` for the maximum available tokens for a
given model. Defaults to `inf`.

types.description: Realtime session object configuration.

types.name: RealtimeSessionCreateRequestGaModel
types.description: The Realtime model used for this session.

types.name: RealtimeSessionCreateRequestGaAudio
types.description: Configuration for input and output audio.

fields.name: noise_reduction
fields.description:
Configuration for input audio noise reduction. This can be set to `null` to turn off.
Noise reduction filters audio added to the input audio buffer before it is sent to VAD and the model.
Filtering the audio can improve VAD and turn detection accuracy (reducing false positives) and model performance by improving perception of the input audio.

types.name: RealtimeAudioFormatsAudioPcm

fields.name: type
fields.description: The audio format. Always `audio/pcm`.

fields.name: rate
fields.description: The sample rate of the audio. Always `24000`.

types.description: The PCM audio format. Only a 24kHz sample rate is supported.

types.name: RealtimeAudioFormatsAudioPcmu

fields.name: type
fields.description: The audio format. Always `audio/pcmu`.

types.description: The G.711 μ-law format.

types.name: RealtimeAudioFormatsAudioPcma

fields.name: type
fields.description: The audio format. Always `audio/pcma`.

types.description: The G.711 A-law format.

fields.name: model
fields.description: The model to use for transcription. Current options are `whisper-1`, `gpt-4o-mini-transcribe`, `gpt-4o-mini-transcribe-2025-12-15`, `gpt-4o-transcribe`, `gpt-4o-transcribe-diarize`, and `gpt-realtime-whisper`. Use `gpt-4o-transcribe-diarize` when you need diarization with speaker labels.

fields.name: language
fields.description:
The language of the input audio. Supplying the input language in
[ISO-639-1](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/List_of_ISO_639-1_codes) (e.g. `en`) format
will improve accuracy and latency.

fields.name: prompt
fields.description:
An optional text to guide the model's style or continue a previous audio
segment.
For `whisper-1`, the [prompt is a list of keywords](/docs/guides/speech-to-text#prompting).
For `gpt-4o-transcribe` models (excluding `gpt-4o-transcribe-diarize`), the prompt is a free text string, for example "expect words related to technology".
Prompt is not supported with `gpt-realtime-whisper` in GA Realtime sessions.

fields.name: delay
fields.description:
Controls how long the model waits before emitting transcription text.
Higher values can improve transcription accuracy at the cost of latency.
Only supported with `gpt-realtime-whisper` in GA Realtime sessions.

types.name: AudioTranscriptionModel
types.description: The model to use for transcription. Current options are `whisper-1`, `gpt-4o-mini-transcribe`, `gpt-4o-mini-transcribe-2025-12-15`, `gpt-4o-transcribe`, `gpt-4o-transcribe-diarize`, and `gpt-realtime-whisper`. Use `gpt-4o-transcribe-diarize` when you need diarization with speaker labels.

types.name: AudioTranscriptionDelay
types.description:
Controls how long the model waits before emitting transcription text.
Higher values can improve transcription accuracy at the cost of latency.
Only supported with `gpt-realtime-whisper` in GA Realtime sessions.

types.name: RealtimeSessionCreateRequestGaAudioInputNoiseReduction
types.description:
Configuration for input audio noise reduction. This can be set to `null` to turn off.
Noise reduction filters audio added to the input audio buffer before it is sent to VAD and the model.
Filtering the audio can improve VAD and turn detection accuracy (reducing false positives) and model performance by improving perception of the input audio.

types.name: NoiseReductionType
types.description: Type of noise reduction. `near_field` is for close-talking microphones such as headphones, `far_field` is for far-field microphones such as laptop or conference room microphones.

types.name: RealtimeTurnDetection
types.description:
Configuration for turn detection, ether Server VAD or Semantic VAD. This can be set to `null` to turn off, in which case the client must manually trigger model response.

Server VAD means that the model will detect the start and end of speech based on audio volume and respond at the end of user speech.

Semantic VAD is more advanced and uses a turn detection model (in conjunction with VAD) to semantically estimate whether the user has finished speaking, then dynamically sets a timeout based on this probability. For example, if user audio trails off with "uhhm", the model will score a low probability of turn end and wait longer for the user to continue speaking. This can be useful for more natural conversations, but may have a higher latency.

For `gpt-realtime-whisper` transcription sessions, turn detection must be
set to `null`; VAD is not supported.

types.name: RealtimeTurnDetectionServerVad

fields.name: type
fields.description: Type of turn detection, `server_vad` to turn on simple Server VAD.

fields.name: threshold
fields.description:
Used only for `server_vad` mode. Activation threshold for VAD (0.0 to 1.0), this defaults to 0.5. A
higher threshold will require louder audio to activate the model, and
thus might perform better in noisy environments.

fields.name: prefix_padding_ms
fields.description:
Used only for `server_vad` mode. Amount of audio to include before the VAD detected speech (in
milliseconds). Defaults to 300ms.

fields.name: silence_duration_ms
fields.description:
Used only for `server_vad` mode. Duration of silence to detect speech stop (in milliseconds). Defaults
to 500ms. With shorter values the model will respond more quickly,
but may jump in on short pauses from the user.

fields.name: create_response
fields.description:
Whether or not to automatically generate a response when a VAD stop event occurs. If `interrupt_response` is set to `false` this may fail to create a response if the model is already responding.

If both `create_response` and `interrupt_response` are set to `false`, the model will never respond automatically but VAD events will still be emitted.

fields.name: interrupt_response
fields.description:
Whether or not to automatically interrupt (cancel) any ongoing response with output to the default
conversation (i.e. `conversation` of `auto`) when a VAD start event occurs. If `true` then the response will be cancelled, otherwise it will continue until complete.

If both `create_response` and `interrupt_response` are set to `false`, the model will never respond automatically but VAD events will still be emitted.

types.description: Server-side voice activity detection (VAD) which flips on when user speech is detected and off after a period of silence.

types.name: RealtimeTurnDetectionSemanticVad

fields.name: type
fields.description: Type of turn detection, `semantic_vad` to turn on Semantic VAD.

fields.name: eagerness
fields.description: Used only for `semantic_vad` mode. The eagerness of the model to respond. `low` will wait longer for the user to continue speaking, `high` will respond more quickly. `auto` is the default and is equivalent to `medium`. `low`, `medium`, and `high` have max timeouts of 8s, 4s, and 2s respectively.

fields.name: create_response
fields.description: Whether or not to automatically generate a response when a VAD stop event occurs.

fields.name: interrupt_response
fields.description:
Whether or not to automatically interrupt any ongoing response with output to the default
conversation (i.e. `conversation` of `auto`) when a VAD start event occurs.

types.description: Server-side semantic turn detection which uses a model to determine when the user has finished speaking.

types.name: RealtimeTurnDetectionSemanticVadEagerness
types.description: Used only for `semantic_vad` mode. The eagerness of the model to respond. `low` will wait longer for the user to continue speaking, `high` will respond more quickly. `auto` is the default and is equivalent to `medium`. `low`, `medium`, and `high` have max timeouts of 8s, 4s, and 2s respectively.

fields.name: speed
fields.description:
The speed of the model's spoken response as a multiple of the original speed.
1.0 is the default speed. 0.25 is the minimum speed. 1.5 is the maximum speed. This value can only be changed in between model turns, not while a response is in progress.

This parameter is a post-processing adjustment to the audio after it is generated, it's
also possible to prompt the model to speak faster or slower.

types.name: VoiceIdsOrCustomVoice
types.description: A built-in voice name or a custom voice reference.

types.name: VoiceIdsOrCustomVoiceVariant1

fields.name: id
fields.description: The custom voice ID, e.g. `voice_1234`.

types.description: Custom voice reference.

types.name: RealtimeSessionCreateRequestGaTracing
types.description:
Realtime API can write session traces to the [Traces Dashboard](https://platform.openai.com/logs?api=traces). Set to null to disable tracing. Once
tracing is enabled for a session, the configuration cannot be modified.

`auto` will create a trace for the session with default values for the
workflow name, group id, and metadata.

types.name: RealtimeSessionCreateRequestGaTracingTracingConfiguration

fields.name: workflow_name
fields.description:
The name of the workflow to attach to this trace. This is used to
name the trace in the Traces Dashboard.

fields.name: group_id
fields.description:
The group id to attach to this trace to enable filtering and
grouping in the Traces Dashboard.

fields.name: metadata
fields.description:
The arbitrary metadata to attach to this trace to enable
filtering in the Traces Dashboard.

types.description: Granular configuration for tracing.

fields.name: type
fields.description: The type of the tool, i.e. `function`.

fields.name: name
fields.description: The name of the function.

fields.name: description
fields.description:
The description of the function, including guidance on when and how
to call it, and guidance about what to tell the user when calling
(if anything).

fields.name: parameters
fields.description: Parameters of the function in JSON Schema.

types.name: McpTool

fields.name: type
fields.description: The type of the MCP tool. Always `mcp`.

fields.name: server_label
fields.description: A label for this MCP server, used to identify it in tool calls.

fields.name: server_url
fields.description:
The URL for the MCP server. One of `server_url` or `connector_id` must be
provided.

fields.name: connector_id
fields.description:
Identifier for service connectors, like those available in ChatGPT. One of
`server_url` or `connector_id` must be provided. Learn more about service
connectors [here](/docs/guides/tools-remote-mcp#connectors).

Currently supported `connector_id` values are:

- Dropbox: `connector_dropbox`
- Gmail: `connector_gmail`
- Google Calendar: `connector_googlecalendar`
- Google Drive: `connector_googledrive`
- Microsoft Teams: `connector_microsoftteams`
- Outlook Calendar: `connector_outlookcalendar`
- Outlook Email: `connector_outlookemail`
- SharePoint: `connector_sharepoint`

fields.name: authorization
fields.description:
An OAuth access token that can be used with a remote MCP server, either
with a custom MCP server URL or a service connector. Your application
must handle the OAuth authorization flow and provide the token here.

fields.name: server_description
fields.description: Optional description of the MCP server, used to provide more context.

fields.name: defer_loading
fields.description: Whether this MCP tool is deferred and discovered via tool search.

types.description:
Give the model access to additional tools via remote Model Context Protocol
(MCP) servers. [Learn more about MCP](/docs/guides/tools-remote-mcp).

types.name: McpToolConnectorId
types.description:
Identifier for service connectors, like those available in ChatGPT. One of
`server_url` or `connector_id` must be provided. Learn more about service
connectors [here](/docs/guides/tools-remote-mcp#connectors).

Currently supported `connector_id` values are:

- Dropbox: `connector_dropbox`
- Gmail: `connector_gmail`
- Google Calendar: `connector_googlecalendar`
- Google Drive: `connector_googledrive`
- Microsoft Teams: `connector_microsoftteams`
- Outlook Calendar: `connector_outlookcalendar`
- Outlook Email: `connector_outlookemail`
- SharePoint: `connector_sharepoint`

types.name: McpToolAllowedTools
types.description: List of allowed tool names or a filter object.

types.name: McpToolFilter

fields.name: tool_names
fields.description: List of allowed tool names.

fields.name: read_only
fields.description:
Indicates whether or not a tool modifies data or is read-only. If an
MCP server is [annotated with `readOnlyHint`](https://modelcontextprotocol.io/specification/2025-06-18/schema#toolannotations-readonlyhint),
it will match this filter.

types.description: A filter object to specify which tools are allowed.

types.name: McpToolRequireApproval
types.description: Specify which of the MCP server's tools require approval.

types.name: McpToolRequireApprovalMcpToolApprovalFilter
types.description:
Specify which of the MCP server's tools require approval. Can be
`always`, `never`, or a filter object associated with tools
that require approval.

types.name: McpToolRequireApprovalMcpToolApprovalSetting
types.description:
Specify a single approval policy for all tools. One of `always` or
`never`. When set to `always`, all tools will require approval. When
set to `never`, all tools will not require approval.

types.name: RealtimeSessionCreateRequestGaToolChoice
types.description:
How the model chooses tools. Provide one of the string modes or force a specific
function/MCP tool.

types.name: ToolChoiceOptions
types.description:
Controls which (if any) tool is called by the model.

`none` means the model will not call any tool and instead generates a message.

`auto` means the model can pick between generating a message or calling one or
more tools.

`required` means the model must call one or more tools.

types.name: ToolChoiceFunction

fields.name: type
fields.description: For function calling, the type is always `function`.

fields.name: name
fields.description: The name of the function to call.

types.description: Use this option to force the model to call a specific function.

types.name: ToolChoiceMcp

fields.name: type
fields.description: For MCP tools, the type is always `mcp`.

fields.name: server_label
fields.description: The label of the MCP server to use.

types.description: Use this option to force the model to call a specific tool on a remote MCP server.

types.name: RealtimeReasoning
types.description: Configuration for reasoning-capable Realtime models such as `gpt-realtime-2`.

types.name: RealtimeReasoningEffort
types.description:
Constrains effort on reasoning for reasoning-capable Realtime models such as
`gpt-realtime-2`.

types.name: RealtimeSessionCreateRequestGaMaxOutputTokens
types.description:
Maximum number of output tokens for a single assistant response,
inclusive of tool calls. Provide an integer between 1 and 4096 to
limit output tokens, or `inf` for the maximum available tokens for a
given model. Defaults to `inf`.

types.name: RealtimeTruncation
types.description:
When the number of tokens in a conversation exceeds the model's input token limit, the conversation be truncated, meaning messages (starting from the oldest) will not be included in the model's context. A 32k context model with 4,096 max output tokens can only include 28,224 tokens in the context before truncation occurs.

Clients can configure truncation behavior to truncate with a lower max token limit, which is an effective way to control token usage and cost.

Truncation will reduce the number of cached tokens on the next turn (busting the cache), since messages are dropped from the beginning of the context. However, clients can also configure truncation to retain messages up to a fraction of the maximum context size, which will reduce the need for future truncations and thus improve the cache rate.

Truncation can be disabled entirely, which means the server will never truncate but would instead return an error if the conversation exceeds the model's input token limit.

types.name: RealtimeTruncationString
types.description: The truncation strategy to use for the session. `auto` is the default truncation strategy. `disabled` will disable truncation and emit errors when the conversation exceeds the input token limit.

types.name: RealtimeTruncationRetentionRatio

fields.name: type
fields.description: Use retention ratio truncation.

fields.name: retention_ratio
fields.description: Fraction of post-instruction conversation tokens to retain (`0.0` - `1.0`) when the conversation exceeds the input token limit. Setting this to `0.8` means that messages will be dropped until 80% of the maximum allowed tokens are used. This helps reduce the frequency of truncations and improve cache rates.

fields.name: token_limits
fields.description: Optional custom token limits for this truncation strategy. If not provided, the model's default token limits will be used.

types.description: Retain a fraction of the conversation tokens when the conversation exceeds the input token limit. This allows you to amortize truncations across multiple turns, which can help improve cached token usage.

types.name: RealtimeTruncationRetentionRatioTokenLimits

fields.name: post_instructions
fields.description: Maximum tokens allowed in the conversation after instructions (which including tool definitions). For example, setting this to 5,000 would mean that truncation would occur when the conversation exceeds 5,000 tokens after instructions. This cannot be higher than the model's context window size minus the maximum output tokens.

types.description: Optional custom token limits for this truncation strategy. If not provided, the model's default token limits will be used.

types.name: Prompt

fields.name: id
fields.description: The unique identifier of the prompt template to use.

types.description:
Reference to a prompt template and its variables.
[Learn more](/docs/guides/text?api-mode=responses#reusable-prompts).

types.name: InputTextContent

fields.name: type
fields.description: The type of the input item. Always `input_text`.

fields.name: text
fields.description: The text input to the model.

types.description: A text input to the model.

types.name: InputImageContent

fields.name: type
fields.description: The type of the input item. Always `input_image`.

types.description: An image input to the model. Learn about [image inputs](/docs/guides/vision).

types.name: InputFileContent

fields.name: type
fields.description: The type of the input item. Always `input_file`.

fields.name: filename
fields.description: The name of the file to be sent to the model.

fields.name: file_data
fields.description: The content of the file to be sent to the model.

fields.name: file_url
fields.description: The URL of the file to be sent to the model.

types.description: A file input to the model.

types.name: RealtimeTranscriptionSessionCreateRequestGa

fields.name: type
fields.description: The type of session to create. Always `transcription` for transcription sessions.

fields.name: audio
fields.description: Configuration for input and output audio.

fields.name: include
fields.description:
Additional fields to include in server outputs.

`item.input_audio_transcription.logprobs`: Include logprobs for input audio transcription.

types.description: Realtime transcription session object configuration.

The default system instructions (i.e. system message) prepended to model calls. This field allows the client to guide the model on desired responses. The model can be instructed on response content and format, (e.g. "be extremely succinct", "act friendly", "here are examples of good responses") and on audio behavior (e.g. "talk quickly", "inject emotion into your voice", "laugh frequently"). The instructions are not guaranteed to be followed by the model, but they provide guidance to the model on the desired behavior.

fields.name: threshold

Activation threshold for VAD (0.0 to 1.0), this defaults to 0.5. A
higher threshold will require louder audio to activate the model, and
thus might perform better in noisy environments.

fields.name: prefix_padding_ms
fields.description:
Amount of audio to include before the VAD detected speech (in
milliseconds). Defaults to 300ms.

fields.name: silence_duration_ms
fields.description:
Duration of silence to detect speech stop (in milliseconds). Defaults

types.description:
Configuration for turn detection. Can be set to `null` to turn off. Server
VAD means that the model will detect the start and end of speech based on
audio volume and respond at the end of user speech. For `gpt-realtime-whisper`, this must be `null`; VAD is not supported.

methods.description:
Create a Realtime client secret with an associated session configuration.

Client secrets are short-lived tokens that can be passed to a client app,
such as a web frontend or mobile client, which grants access to the Realtime API without
leaking your main API key. You can configure a custom TTL for each client secret.

You can also attach session configuration options to the client secret, which will be
applied to any sessions created using that client secret, but these can also be overridden
by the client connection.

[Learn more about authentication with client secrets over WebRTC](/docs/guides/realtime-webrtc).

Returns the created client secret and the effective session object. The client secret is a string that looks like `ek_1234`.

requestBody.description: Create a client secret with the given session configuration.
